body.title: CLOSED: Test-suite flakes from test-ordering/shared-state
body.result: added per-test fixtures to clear shared state in both flaky test files
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Two tests fail non-deterministically depending on test execution order, when running `pytest -x` at the whole-suite level. Observed 2026-04-24:
- Run A with my goal_adopt change: test_pr_accretion.py::test_pr_recent_accretions_orders_newest_first failed at position ~2638. Passes in isolation. Passes when the whole test_pr_accretion.py file runs alone.
- Run B without my change (git stash, same commit): test_pe_chain_qwen_tier.py::TestPeChainRoutesToQwen::test_pe_plan_logs_qwen_model failed at position ~2487. Different test entirely.

Conclusion: shared state (Postgres DB rows, module-level singletons, TWM observations) leaks between tests. Test A that writes state → test B reads it and gets wrong answer. This has been masking real failures and making `pytest -x` unreliable as a gate. Folds into the broader T-test-postgres-schema (GH#511) work, but smaller scope: for the two named tests specifically, add per-test setup/teardown that clears the state they depend on.

**Affected files:** tests/test_pr_accretion.py, tests/test_pe_chain_qwen_tier.py, possibly tests/conftest.py (for shared teardown). **Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — identify the shared-state source for each test, add per-test setup/teardown. OUT — the full test schema migration (that's T-test-postgres-schema), fixing every possible cross-test leak (just the two named above). **Test plan:** run the whole suite with `pytest tests/ -q` (no -x) 10 times; both named tests pass every time. Also run with `pytest tests/ --randomly-seed=1234` if randomization is on; still pass.
